# Account AI & Data Governance Manager

Employer CBRE Location Bratislava ', Slovakia Salary Competitive Closing date 29 Jun 2026

View more categoriesView less categories

Save jobClick to add the job to your shortlist

## Job Details

A senior business-led role responsible for building and running CBRE's AI capability on the clients account. The postholder owns the end-to-end AI programme, the data governance framework, and the named QA sign-off before any AI tool reaches client.This is a newly created Programme Manager role, sitting at the intersection of business operations, data strategy, and AI enablement. The role requires commercial credibility, change leadership, and the ability to navigate a complex, regulated client environment.Own the AI Roadmap

- Sets direction, priorities and sequencing of all AI initiatives. Owns ideation to production, chairs the AI Working Group and leads the Champions Network.

Run the QA Gate

- No AI tool reaches client without passing defined acceptance criteria. The Manager is the named sign-off - in partnership with D&T Partner - before any tool goes live.

Lead Tool Adoption

- Owns the transition of each approved tool from pilot into live operations, managing change and embedding adoption across all account functions, regions and team types.

Own Data Governance

- Owns quality, completeness and governance of all account datasets - FM, RE, BI and wider - to ensure the data foundation that AI use cases require to function.

Essential

- Senior account or client-facing role in FM, RE or digital transformation
- Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional change programmes in complex, regulated environments
- Working knowledge of AI and data tools - able to interrogate a product critically, not a coder
- Strong stakeholder management across multiple seniority levels and organisational boundaries

Desirable

- Familiarity with data governance frameworks and quality management principles
- Experience working within or alongside a regulated financial services client
- Prior exposure to AI working groups, innovation programmes or technology adoption initiatives

The CBRE AI CTO appointment is the May 15, 2026 hiring of Anuj Kadyan, a senior partner at McKinsey & Company and co-leader of its Technology Services practice, as CBRE Group's new Chief Technology & Transformation Officer. The hire embeds a McKinsey AI strategist at the top of the world's largest commercial real estate services firm, signaling that CBRE intends to compete on technology and AI, not just brokerage scale, over the next 5 years. For the broader landscape of AI commercial real estate moves, this is one of the most consequential leadership decisions of 2026.

On March 20, 2026, CBRE Group, Inc. (NYSE: CBRE) announced that Anuj Kadyan would join the company as Chief Technology & Transformation Officer effective May 15, 2026. Kadyan is a senior partner at McKinsey & Company, where he co-leads the Technology Services practice. Over the past 17 years he has advised the world's leading AI, cloud, and technology companies on core strategy, M&A, and commercial operations, and has led large-scale AI and technology transformations for major companies across industries. He joined McKinsey in 2009 and has worked across the New Jersey, Atlanta, London, Gurugram, and Delhi offices, and previously led the Telecom, Media & Tech practice in India.
[...]
CBRE Chair and CEO Bob Sulentic framed the move bluntly: "CBRE will benefit significantly from Anuj's extensive experience across technology, transformation and strategy. This combination will be especially powerful in helping us to further differentiate our products and to operate our business more efficiently." Kadyan, for his part, called out the building blocks: "Data, insight and scale, combined with the right strategy, are attributes that will define success in the future." Translation for CRE investors: CBRE intends to turn its data and platform advantage into AI-native product differentiation, and it just hired a senior partner from McKinsey & Company to lead that effort.

highlights:
CBRE, the world’s largest commercial real estate services firm, has long been associated with brokerage, facilities management, and investment sales. But the century-old company—founded in the aftermath of the devastating 1906 San Francisco earthquake—is now being reshaped by another kind of seismic event: the AI data center boom.
[...]
The rise of generative AI has triggered an unprecedented wave of data center construction as companies scramble to secure the computing power needed to train and run large AI models. As hyperscalers race to acquire land, power, and water for massive AI infrastructure projects, CBRE, which is headquartered in Dallas, has quietly become a key player behind the scenes.
[...]
The company says it has secured “dozens” of potential data center sites around the country and is working directly with major tech companies on everything from land entitlements to power and water access. Unlike pure brokerage firms or construction contractors, CBRE increasingly sits across multiple layers of the AI infrastructure buildout.
[...]
The shift is already reshaping CBRE’s business. The company generated more than $3 billion in infrastructure-related revenue in 2025 and nearly $950 million in the first quarter alone. It recently created a dedicated “critical infrastructure services” unit focused on data centers, telecom, and power infrastructure—a business expected to grow more than 60% this year. CBRE also said its data center leasing revenue more than tripled year-over-year in the first quarter.

Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Senior or Principal Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will drive the development of our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.
[...]
Domain Translation: Translate biological and chemical domain knowledge into machine learning objectives, training signals, and evaluation criteria, working closely with domain experts.

You architect new initiatives
[...]
integrate diverse data modalities, guiding the technical direction of cross-functional projects across gRED.

results.title: Standard Chartered Data AI Specialist 2026
highlights:
Standard Chartered Bank is launching its 2026 recruitment drive for the role of Specialist, Data, Analytics & AI – OTCR, based in Chennai. This role is part of the bank’s AI Centre of Excellence (CoE) and focuses on transforming risk management and regulatory compliance through advanced analytics, machine learning, and generative AI. As financial institutions increasingly adopt AI to automate compliance, detect financial crime, and enhance credit risk modeling, Standard Chartered is seeking fresh talent with hands-on exposure to GenAI/LLM projects. The position offers a unique opportunity to work on high-impact AI use cases in a regulated banking environment, bridging the gap between data science and business outcomes.
[...]
in Data Science
[...]
Candidates must hold a degree in a quantitative field such as Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or Engineering. A postgraduate degree or professional qualifications in analytics, risk, or compliance are advantageous. While the role is open to freshers, hands-on exposure to GenAI/LLM projects (even academic POCs or personal projects) is a strong differentiator. The bank values background in solution architecture, data/ML engineering, or advanced analytics. For freshers, relevant internships or capstone projects in financial crime, credit risk, or compliance analytics will give an edge. The bank typically looks for candidates with strong analytical thinking, programming proficiency (Python, R, SQL), and familiarity with ML lifecycle management.
[...]
- Design and deploy advanced analytics, ML, and AI solutions for risk management and regulatory compliance.
- Identify and prioritize high-impact AI/ML use cases including financial crime, credit risk, operational risk, and compliance monitoring.
- Build robust, explainable models that comply with internal policies and external regulations.
- Embed models into business processes, ensuring trust, monitoring, and continuous improvement.
[...]
- Python
- R
- SQL
- Machine Learning (supervised & unsupervised)
- Natural Language Processing
- GenAI/LLM
- MLOps
- Data Governance
[...]
The selection process typically includes an initial resume screening, followed by an online assessment covering quantitative aptitude, logical reasoning, and basic data science concepts. Shortlisted candidates then face a technical interview focusing on Python, SQL, ML algorithms, and GenAI/LLM project experience. A final HR round assesses fit with the bank’s culture and risk awareness. The entire process may take 2-4 weeks. Given the specialized nature, candidates with demonstrable project work in NLP or LLMs have a higher chance of progressing.
[...]
The job description explicitly asks for hands-on exposure to GenAI/LLM projects. In 2025-26, banks are racing to adopt generative AI for document review, automated reporting, and customer interaction. As a fresher, even a small project—like building a chatbot for compliance FAQs or fine-tuning an LLM for summarising legal documents—can significantly boost your application. The bank is looking for candidates who understand not just how to use pre-trained models, but also how to evaluate their outputs for bias, accuracy, and regulatory compliance. This is a niche skill that commands premium salaries and faster career growth.
[...]
Candidates with a degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, or related quantitative fields. Freshers with hands-on experience in GenAI/LLM projects, ML, and advanced analytics are preferred. Postgraduates in analytics or risk-related disciplines have an advantage.
[...]
Interested candidates should apply through the official Standard Chartered careers link provided in the job post. Ensure your resume highlights any GenAI/LLM projects, ML internships, or relevant coursework. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is recommended.
